meta.title: DeepLearning.AI Learner Community Event ft. Jan Jitse Venselaar
meta.description:
deeplearning.ai presents virtual mini-event series for our Learner Community of those taking the AI for Medicine specialization!

#MeetYourMentor: Mentors sharing their personal experiences of breaking into AI
#DeepDive: A deep dive into a specific area that the guest speaker is working on
and more!

This event will be from 10am to 10:30 am on May 14 PDT featuring Jan Jitse Venselaar, and Jan is going to share how they develop digital biomarkers using ML on data gathered from mobile phones (reaction speed, walking ability, eye movement) for MS patients. 

About guest speaker Jan Jitse Venselaar:
Jan studied physics and mathematics at Utrecht University in the Netherlands. After finishing his PhD in 2012 there, he did postdoctoral research at Caltech in the US and at Gottingen university in Germany.
He was an engineer and architect at ASML, working on novel metrology solutions for the semiconductor industry using machine learning. At the start of 2020 he became a senior data scientist at Orikami, developing solutions for monitoring the progress of disease in chronic MS patients.
In his spare time he likes tinkering with computers and running.

Course 1 and 2 of the AI For Medicine Specialization is available now on Coursera! The third course will be available by the end of May.
 
Enroll in the AI For Medicine Specialization here:  https://bit.ly/2UBOUEO
Check out all our courses: https://bit.ly/39yz95P
